key_insight: A lightweight alignment layer can connect frozen H&E histopathology and RNA-seq models so gene-set and pathway activity can be inferred directly from slide images without new sequencing or end-to-end retraining.
clinical_relevance: This could help stratify tumors and estimate molecular states from routinely available pathology slides, supporting faster and cheaper precision-oncology workflows.
method: Contrastive learning aligns frozen histopathology and RNA-seq foundation models.
Open-vocabulary molecular prompting queries H&E slides with gene-set signatures.
Data-efficient domain adaptation enables transfer across cohorts without retraining the full model.
evidence_signal: In a multi-cancer cohort of 1,720 samples, the approach achieved a 25-fold retrieval improvement over baseline methods and was validated on external cohorts and the POSEIDON clinical trial.
watch_item: Predictions remain limited for pathways with little or no morphological footprint, so performance may vary by biology and should be validated before use in new settings.
paper_title: Data-Efficient Multimodal Alignment for Histopathology-based Molecular Prediction
